Provides information about how to authenticate to the docker registry containing
the custom base image.

gcp_oidc_service_account.description: GCP service account name for OIDC authentication.

This enum will express all the types of registry
authentication we support.

DockerServer.description: Deploy a custom Docker image that has its own HTTP server, without writing a Model class.

start_command.description: The command to start the server. Required when no_build is not true.
start_command.title: Start Command

server_port.description: The port where the server runs. Port 8080 is reserved by Baseten's internal reverse proxy and cannot be used.
server_port.title: Server Port

predict_endpoint.description: The endpoint for inference requests. This is mapped to Baseten's /predict route.
predict_endpoint.title: Predict Endpoint

readiness_endpoint.description: The endpoint for readiness probes. Determines when the container can accept traffic.
readiness_endpoint.title: Readiness Endpoint

liveness_endpoint.description: The endpoint for liveness probes. Determines if the container needs to be restarted.
liveness_endpoint.title: Liveness Endpoint

run_as_user_id.description: The Linux UID to run the server process as inside the container. Use this when your base image expects a specific non-root user (for example, NVIDIA NIM containers).
run_as_user_id.title: Run As User Id

no_build.description: Skip the build step and deploy the base image as-is. Baseten copies the image to its container registry without running docker build or modifying the image in any way.
no_build.title: No Build

DockerServer.title: DockerServer

ExternalData.description:
[Experimental] External data is data that is not contained in the Truss folder.

Typically, this will be data stored remotely. This data is guaranteed to be made
available under the data directory of the truss.
ExternalData.title: ExternalData

ExternalDataItem.description:
A piece of remote data, to be made available to the Truss at serving time.

Remote data is downloaded and stored under Truss's data directory. Care should be taken
to avoid conflicts. This will get precedence if there's overlap.

url.description: URL to download the data from. Currently only files are allowed.
url.title: Url

local_data_path.description: Path relative to the data directory where the remote file will be downloaded.
local_data_path.title: Local Data Path

backend.description: Download backend to use. Defaults to 'http_public'.
backend.title: Backend

name.description: Optional name for the download. Path relative to data directory.
name.title: Name

ExternalDataItem.title: ExternalDataItem

HealthChecks.description: Custom health check configuration for your deployments.

restart_check_delay_seconds.description: The delay in seconds before starting restart checks. Defaults to platform-determined value when not set.
restart_check_delay_seconds.title: Restart Check Delay Seconds

restart_threshold_seconds.description: The time in seconds after which an unhealthy instance is restarted. Defaults to platform-determined value when not set.
restart_threshold_seconds.title: Restart Threshold Seconds

stop_traffic_threshold_seconds.description: The time in seconds after which traffic is stopped to an unhealthy instance. Defaults to platform-determined value when not set.
stop_traffic_threshold_seconds.title: Stop Traffic Threshold Seconds

startup_threshold_seconds.description: The time in seconds to wait for a model to start before marking it as unhealthy. Defaults to platform-determined value when not set.
startup_threshold_seconds.title: Startup Threshold Seconds

HealthChecks.title: HealthChecks

ModelRepoSourceKind.description: syned with `pub enum ResolutionType` in truss-transfer
ModelRepoSourceKind.title: ModelRepoSourceKind

ModelServer.description:
To determine the image builder path for trusses built from alternative server backends.
This enum is also used to gate development deployments to BasetenRemote
https://github.com/basetenlabs/truss/blob/7505c17a2ddd4a6fa626b9126772999dc8f3fa86/truss/remote/baseten/remote.py#L56-L57
ModelServer.title: ModelServer

Resources.description: Compute resources that your model needs, including CPU, memory, and GPU resources.

cpu.description: CPU resources needed, expressed as either a raw number or millicpus. For example, 500m is half of a CPU core.
cpu.title: Cpu

memory.description: CPU RAM needed, expressed as a number with units. Units include Gi (Gibibytes), G (Gigabytes), Mi (Mebibytes), and M (Megabytes).
memory.title: Memory

accelerator.description: The GPU type for your instance. To request multiple GPUs, use the ':' operator (e.g. L4:4).

instance_type.description: The full SKU name for the instance type. When specified, cpu, memory, and accelerator fields are ignored.
instance_type.title: Instance Type

node_count.description: Number of nodes for multi-node deployments.
node_count.title: Node Count

Resources.title: Resources

Runtime.description: Runtime settings for your model instance.

predict_concurrency.description: The number of concurrent requests that can run in your model's predict method. Increase this if your model supports parallelism.
predict_concurrency.title: Predict Concurrency

streaming_read_timeout.description: The timeout in seconds for streaming read operations.
streaming_read_timeout.title: Streaming Read Timeout

enable_tracing_data.description: If true, enables trace data export with built-in OTEL instrumentation. May add performance overhead.
enable_tracing_data.title: Enable Tracing Data

enable_debug_logs.description: If true, sets the Truss server log level to DEBUG instead of INFO.
enable_debug_logs.title: Enable Debug Logs

transport.description: The transport protocol for your model. Supports http (default), websocket, and grpc.
transport.title: Transport

is_websocket_endpoint.description: DEPRECATED. Do not set manually. Automatically inferred from transport.kind == websocket.
is_websocket_endpoint.title: Is Websocket Endpoint

truss_server_version_override.description: By default, truss servers are built from the same release as the CLI used to push. This field allows specifying a pinned/specific version instead.
truss_server_version_override.title: Truss Server Version Override

Runtime.title: Runtime

training_job_id.description: The training job id that the artifact reference belongs to.
training_job_id.title: Training Job Id

paths.description: The paths of the files to download which can contain * or ?.
paths.title: Paths

kv_cache_free_gpu_mem_fraction.title: Kv Cache Free Gpu Mem Fraction

TrussTRTQuantizationConfiguration.description:
Configuration for quantization of TRT models

Args:
    calib_size (int, optional): Size of calibration dataset. Defaults to 1024.
        recommended to increase for production runs (e.g. 1536), or decrease e.g. to 256 for quick testing.
    calib_dataset (str, optional): Hugginface dataset to use for calibration. Defaults to 'cnn_dailymail'.
        uses split='train' and  quantized based on 'text' column.
    calib_max_seq_length (int, optional): Maximum sequence length for calibration. Defaults to 2048.
TrussTRTQuantizationConfiguration.title: TrussTRTQuantizationConfiguration

Weights.description: List of weights sources for the new weights API.
Weights.title: Weights

WeightsAuth.description:
Authentication configuration for a weights source.

This can be used to specify OIDC-based authentication for cloud storage sources,
or a Baseten secret name for access key authentication.

aws_oidc_role_arn.description: AWS IAM role ARN for OIDC authentication.
aws_oidc_role_arn.title: Aws Oidc Role Arn

gcp_oidc_service_account.description: GCP service account name for OIDC authentication.
gcp_oidc_service_account.title: Gcp Oidc Service Account

gcp_oidc_workload_id_provider.description: GCP workload identity provider for OIDC authentication.
gcp_oidc_workload_id_provider.title: Gcp Oidc Workload Id Provider

auth_method.description: Authentication method for downloading weights from the source.

auth_secret_name.description: Baseten secret name containing credentials for accessing the source.
auth_secret_name.title: Auth Secret Name

WeightsAuth.title: WeightsAuth

WeightsSource.description:
Configuration for a weights source in the new weights API.

Uses a URI-based `source` field with a required scheme prefix:
- hf:// -> HuggingFace (e.g., "hf://meta-llama/Llama-2-7b" or "hf://meta-llama/Llama-2-7b@main")
- s3:// -> AWS S3 (e.g., "s3://bucket/path")
- gs:// -> Google Cloud Storage (e.g., "gs://bucket/path")
- azure:// -> Azure Blob Storage (e.g., "azure://account/container/path")
- r2:// -> CloudFlare R2 Storage (e.g., "r2://account_id.bucket/path")
- https:// -> Direct URL download (e.g., "https://example.com/model.bin")

For HuggingFace sources, you can specify a revision (branch, tag, or commit SHA)
using the @{rev} suffix: "hf://owner/repo@revision"

Authentication can be specified either:
- Using the `auth` section (required for OIDC):
    auth:
      auth_method: AWS_OIDC
      aws_oidc_role_arn: <role_arn>
      aws_oidc_region: <region>
- Using `auth_secret_name` at the top level (or in the `auth` section)

source.description: URI with scheme prefix. Use hf://, s3://, gs://, azure://, r2://, or https://. For HuggingFace, use @revision suffix (e.g., hf://owner/repo@main).
source.title: Source

mount_location.description: Absolute path where weights will be mounted at runtime.
mount_location.title: Mount Location

auth.description: Authentication configuration for accessing the weights source.

auth_secret_name.description: Baseten secret name containing credentials. Can also be specified in auth.auth_secret_name.
auth_secret_name.title: Auth Secret Name

WeightsSource.title: WeightsSource

description: Configuration for a Truss model deployment.

model_name.description: The name of your model. This is displayed in the model details page in the Baseten UI.
model_name.title: Model Name

model_metadata.description: A flexible field for additional metadata. The entire config file is available to your model at runtime.

example_model_input.description: Sample input that populates the Baseten playground.

examples.prompt: What is the meaning of life?

model_metadata.title: Model Metadata

examples_filename.description: Path to a file containing example model inputs.
examples_filename.title: Examples Filename

data_dir.description: The folder for data files in your Truss.
data_dir.title: Data Dir

external_data.description: External data to be downloaded and made available under the data directory at serving time.

external_package_dirs.description: Use external_package_dirs to access custom packages located outside your Truss. This lets multiple Trusses share the same package.
external_package_dirs.title: External Package Dirs

base_image.description: Use a custom Docker base image instead of the default Truss image.

requirements_file.description: Path to a dependency file. Supports requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, and uv.lock. Mutually exclusive with 'requirements'.
requirements_file.title: Requirements File

requirements.description: A list of Python dependencies in pip requirements file format. Mutually exclusive with 'requirements_file'.
requirements.title: Requirements

system_packages.description: System packages that you would typically install using apt on a Debian operating system.
system_packages.title: System Packages

environment_variables.description: Key-value pairs exposed to the environment that the model executes in. Do not store secret values here.
environment_variables.title: Environment Variables

secrets.description: Declare secrets your model needs at runtime, such as API keys or access tokens. Use null as a placeholder; store actual values in your organization settings.
secrets.title: Secrets

build_commands.description: A list of shell commands to run during Docker build. These commands execute after system packages and Python requirements are installed.
build_commands.title: Build Commands

docker_server.description: Deploy a custom Docker image that has its own HTTP server, without writing a Model class.

model_cache.description: Deprecated. Use 'weights' instead. Bundle model weights into your image at build time.

weights.description: Configure Baseten Delivery Network (BDN) for model weight delivery with multi-tier caching.

trt_llm.description: TensorRT-LLM configuration for optimized LLM inference.

training_checkpoints.description: Configuration for deploying from training checkpoints.

model_module_dir.description: The folder containing your model class.
model_module_dir.title: Model Module Dir

model_class_name.description: The name of the class that defines your Truss model. This class must implement at least a predict method.
model_class_name.title: Model Class Name

bundled_packages_dir.description: The folder for custom packages in your Truss.
bundled_packages_dir.title: Bundled Packages Dir

live_reload.description: If true, changes to your model code are automatically reloaded without restarting the server.
live_reload.title: Live Reload

apply_library_patches.description: Whether to apply library patches for improved compatibility.
apply_library_patches.title: Apply Library Patches

title: TrussConfig
